betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Spatial Storage Cells§f:
Spatial Storage Cells (appliedenergistics2:spatial_storage_cell_2_cubed, 16_cubed, 128_cubed) are the §emost powerful storage device in AE2§f. They store an entire 3D volume of blocks, with all tile entities and inventories intact.

§6Crafting Recipe (network/cells/spatial_storage_cell_2.json)§f:
The 2³ Spatial Storage Cell is crafted with:
- 1× ME Storage Housing (appliedenergistics2:material:36) at center
- 1× §62³§r Spatial Component (appliedenergistics2:material:32)
- 1× Redstone (vanilla)
- 1× Quartz Glass (appliedenergistics2:quartz_glass)
- 4× Iron Ingot (vanilla, in corners)
- Yields 1× §62³§r Spatial Storage Cell

§6Spatial Storage Cell Tiers§f:
- §b2³ Spatial Cell§f (appliedenergistics2:spatial_storage_cell_2_cubed): 2×2×2 cube (8 blocks)
- §b16³ Spatial Cell§f: 16×16×16 cube (4096 blocks)
- §b128³ Spatial Cell§f: 128×128×128 cube (2,097,152 blocks, late-game)

§6Direct Descendants of 2³ Spatial Storage Cell (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 1 item that uses the 2³ Cell:
- Machine Controller (modularmachinery) — modular machinery multiblock controller

§6How to Use Spatial Storage§f:
1. Place the Spatial Cell in the world
2. Build a structure within the cell's volume (a 2×2×2 cube for a 2³ cell)
3. Right-click the cell with a §bSpatial Containment Tool§f to capture the structure
4. The structure is removed from the world and stored in the cell
5. Right-click the cell on a new location to release the structure

§6What Gets Stored§f:
Spatial cells store:
- §bAll blocks§f in the volume (any block type)
- §bAll tile entities§f (chests, machines, signs, command blocks)
- §bAll inventories§f (chest contents, machine buffers)
- §bAll block states§f (orientation, color, etc.)
- §bAll NBT data§f (custom names, enchantments, etc.)

§6Crafting Ingredients (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 5 ingredient items:
- ME Storage Housing (AE2)
- 2³ Spatial Component (AE2)
- Quartz Glass (AE2)
- Redstone (vanilla)
- Iron Ingot (vanilla)

§6Why Spatial Cells Matter§f:
Spatial cells are the §emost flexible storage in AE2§f:
- Move entire bases
- Backup critical structures
- Create portable infrastructure
- Transport quarried landscapes

§6Limitations§f:
- §bFixed volume§f: a 2³ cell cannot store a 3×3 structure
- §bSingle structure per cell§f: each cell holds one snapshot
- §bPower required to release§f: releasing a structure needs AE power
- §bSome blocks may not store§f: certain tile entities (e.g. some mod blocks) may have issues

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30167 (Spatial Components) — the cell requires a spatial component.

§eTip§f: For a starter backup, capture a §bsmall house or workshop§f in a 2³ or 16³ cell. Keep the cell in a safe place (vault, ender chest) for emergencies.

§eTip§f: Use spatial cells for §bquarry output transport§f. Capture a quarry in a 16³ cell, release it at your processing facility.

§cWarning§f: Capturing a structure in a spatial cell requires §bno other players or entities in the area§f. Hostile mobs or other players in the volume will cause capture failures.
